old_claim.note: Seed Selection provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: Grafting propagates joined vegetative tissues and does not technically depend on selecting or reproducing plants from seed.

source_shape.source_locator: Summary: ancient Greek, Chinese, and Biblical references show people cutting and joining plants by at least the fifth century BCE.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Written evidence places plant grafting in Europe, the Middle East, and Asia by at least the fifth century BCE.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The scoped evidence establishes the milestone without the removed dependency. Grafting propagates joined vegetative tissues and does not technically depend on selecting or reproducing plants from seed.

ontology_before: orchard_grafting treated seed_selection as a direct causal prerequisite.
ontology_after: orchard_grafting is scoped to the directly evidenced mechanism and no longer claims seed_selection as a prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-structural-accuracy-batch: orchard_grafting no longer directly depends on seed_selection.
would_reject_if: A reliable source demonstrates that every implementation within the current orchard_grafting scope technically requires seed_selection.
short_reason: Grafting propagates joined vegetative tissues and does not technically depend on selecting or reproducing plants from seed.
